background: Suppose you plan to invest equal amounts of money in each of five business ventures. If you have 20 ventures from which to make the selection, you need to determine the number of different samples of five ventures that can be selected from the 20.

task.query: How many different samples of 5 ventures can be selected from 20 ventures?
task.answer: For this example, $N=20$ and $n=5$. Then the number of different samples of 5 that can be selected from the 20 ventures is $$ \begin{aligned} \binom{20}{5} & =\frac{20!}{5!(20-5)!}=\frac{20!}{5!15!} \\ & =\frac{20 \cdot 19 \cdot 18 \cdot \cdots \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1}{(5 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1)(15 \cdot 14 \cdot 13 \cdots \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1)}=\frac{20 \cdot 19 \cdot 18 \cdot 17 \cdot 16}{5 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1}=15,504 \end{aligned} $$

meta_info.caption: Using the Combinations Rule-Selecting 5 Investments from 20
